svg.title: 7 Practical Components of IPCC Sl.No Experiments 1

section.p: To start using MongoDB, you need to install it on your local machine or in a cloud environment.

ul.li: Download the MongoDB Community Server from the official MongoDB website.
Extract the downloaded zip file to a directory of your choice.
Create a new data directory for MongoDB by running the command `mongod --dbpath /path/to/data`.
Start the MongoDB service by running the command `mongod`.

section.p: The Mongo Shell is a command-line interface for interacting with MongoDB.

ul.li: Connect to a MongoDB instance using the `use` command.
Create a new database using the `use` command.
Insert data into a collection using the `db.collection.insert()` method.

section.p: The MongoDB Atlas is a managed cloud service that provides a scalable and secure data storage solution.

ul.li: Sign up for a MongoDB Atlas account.
Create a new cluster.
Connect to the cluster using the `mongodb+srv://:@.mongodb.net/?retryWrites=true&w=majority` command.

section.p: The MongoDB Node.js Driver is a library that allows you to interact with MongoDB from your Node.js application.

ul.li: Install the MongoDB Node.js Driver using npm or yarn.
Import the driver in your Node.js application.
Connect to a MongoDB instance using the `mongoose.connect()` method.

section.p: The MongoDB Python Driver is a library that allows you to interact with MongoDB from your Python application.

ul.li: Install the MongoDB Python Driver using pip.
Import the driver in your Python application.
Connect to a MongoDB instance using the `mongo.client()` method.

section.p: The MongoDB Java Driver is a library that allows you to interact with MongoDB from your Java application.

ul.li: Install the MongoDB Java Driver using Maven or Gradle.
Import the driver in your Java application.
Connect to a MongoDB instance using the `MongoClient` class.

section.p: The MongoDB Go Driver is a library that allows you to interact with MongoDB from your Go application.

ul.li: Install the MongoDB Go Driver using go-get.
Import the driver in your Go application.
Connect to a MongoDB instance using the `mongo.Client` struct.
